name: Universities & Schools

tools.name: room_utilization
tools.description: Finds rooms consistently booked but empty — no-show booking waste
tools.query:
FROM classroom-bookings
| STATS
    booked_hours = SUM(booking_duration_hours),
    actual_hours = SUM(actual_occupancy_hours)
  BY room_id, building_id, department
| EVAL
    wasted_hours = booked_hours - actual_hours,
    no_show_rate = TO_DOUBLE(booked_hours - actual_hours) / TO_DOUBLE(booked_hours)
| WHERE no_show_rate > 0.35
| SORT wasted_hours DESC
| LIMIT 20

tools.name: software_license_waste
tools.description: Finds software licenses purchased in bulk with very low concurrent usage
tools.query:
FROM software-license-usage
| STATS
    licenses_owned = MAX(license_count),
    peak_concurrent = MAX(concurrent_users),
    avg_daily = AVG(daily_active_users)
  BY software_name, department
| EVAL
    unused_licenses = licenses_owned - peak_concurrent
| WHERE unused_licenses > 10
| SORT unused_licenses DESC
| LIMIT 20

pattern_seeker_prompt:
## ROLE
You are the Pattern Seeker for Ghost Economy Hunter deployed at a university or school.

## ANOMALY TYPES
TYPE 1 — ROOM_BOOKING_WASTE: Rooms booked by departments with consistently low actual attendance.
TYPE 2 — SOFTWARE_LICENSE_WASTE: Software licenses purchased in bulk with low concurrent usage.
TYPE 3 — LAB_EQUIPMENT_IDLE: Expensive lab equipment powered on with no active experiment.

## CONSTRAINTS
Do not flag rooms as waste during exam periods or holidays.
Software usage naturally drops during summer — check semester schedule before flagging.
